initial_claims.description: Reassigning a storage array to an empty array in Solidity 0.4 iterates over all elements to clear their storage slots. If the array has grown unboundedly, this operation will exceed the block gas limit, causing an Out-Of-Gas (OOG) exception and permanently preventing the array from being cleared.
initial_claims.evidence: Line 29: `array = new uint[](0);` clears the storage array. Since `insertNnumbers` allows anyone to increase the array length unboundedly, an attacker can grow the array until clearing it costs more gas than the block limit.

The Defender explicitly agrees with the vulnerability claim, confirming that the unbounded array clearing in Solidity 0.4 leads to an Out-Of-Gas (OOG) Denial of Service. No valid mitigations were presented to invalidate the finding.

The attacker is correct. The previous response was cut off, but the analysis remains the same: there are no mitigations in the code to prevent the array from growing unboundedly, and clearing a large storage array in Solidity 0.4 will iterate over all elements, leading to an Out-Of-Gas (OOG) Denial of Service.
